children.heading: Grant program authorized
children.label: a Grant program authorized
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations to carry out this subpart, the Secretary shall make grants to eligible partnerships (as described in subsection (f)) to support the engagement of underrepresented minority youth and youth who are low-income individuals (as such term is defined in ) in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through outreach and hands-on, experiential-based learning projects that encourage students in kindergarten through grade 12 who are underrepresented minority youth or low-income individuals to pursue careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.section 1058 of this title

children.heading: Minimum grant amount
children.label: b Minimum grant amount
children.text: A grant awarded to a partnership under this subpart shall be for an amount that is not less than $500,000.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: c Duration
children.text: A grant awarded under this subpart shall be for a period of five years.

children.heading: Non-Federal matching share required
children.label: d Non-Federal matching share required
children.text: A partnership receiving a grant under this subpart shall provide, from non-Federal sources, in cash or in-kind, an amount equal to 50 percent of the costs of the project supported by such grant.

children.heading: Distribution of grants
children.label: e Distribution of grants
children.text: In awarding grants under this subpart, the Secretary shall ensure that, to the maximum extent practicable, the projects funded under this subpart are located in diverse geographic regions of the United States.

children.heading: Eligible partnerships
children.label: f Eligible partnerships
children.text: Notwithstanding the general eligibility provision in , eligibility to receive grants under this subpart is limited to partnerships described in paragraph (5) of such section.section 1067g of this title
